exemplar.title: How and to what extent is characterisation used to warn society of the impacts of rape culture in Fennellâs Promising Young Woman?â

detailedExemplarData.title: How and to what extent is characterisation used to warn society of the impacts of rape culture in Fennellâs Promising Young Woman?â

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

strengths: Clear and sustained purpose articulated through section headings and topic sentences
Research question is narrow, precise, and tightly linked to the analysis
Coherent organising principle (four character groupings) maintained throughout
weaknesses: Methodology description lacks full robustness and detail on analytical frameworks
Occasional digressions into plot summary dilute analytical focus
Abrupt ending to methodology section limits clarity on systematic application
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place two elements firmly in the 5â6 band (topic clarity and research question) and one in the upper 3â4 band (methodology). Overall performance aligns with a low-to-mid 5 because the purpose and focus are effective, but the methodological explanation is not yet fully comprehensive.

strengths: Regular and mostly accurate use of subject-specific terminology (e.g., diegetic sound, indirect characterisation)
Solid contextual knowledge of rape-culture discourse and cinematic conventions
Sophisticated intertextual reference (mythological Cassandra) enhances depth
weaknesses: Occasional slips in defining film concepts (e.g., conflating mise-en-scÃ¨ne with costume)
Some claims rely on popular press rather than peer-reviewed scholarship
Terminology application is not consistently precise across sections
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands are judged in the 3â4 band: the student shows good knowledge and use of terminology but falls short of consistently excellent coherence and effective source application, warranting a score of 4.

strengths: Arguments are grounded in a relevant mix of primary and secondary sources
Analysis links cinematic techniques to thematic claims with reasonable depth
Overall line of argument is coherent, culminating in an integrative conclusion
weaknesses: Dependence on non-scholarly sources (Wikipedia, video essays) weakens academic rigour
Some analytical leaps lack empirical support or counter-argument engagement
Discussion occasionally drifts into descriptive summary rather than critical evaluation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three critical-thinking strands fall in the 4â6 band. While the research is mostly relevant and the argument coherent, gaps in source rigour and critical counter-analysis place the work squarely in the mid-band, justifying a score of 5 out of 12.

strengths: Clearly appropriate structure with all required elements (title page, contents, conclusion, works cited)
Consistent pagination and word count presentation
Overall layout, font, and citation style are correctly applied
weaknesses: Inconsistent figure caption formatting and timestamp citation
Erratic line breaks and split headings disrupt visual flow
Reference list indentation and URL/DOI formatting lack uniformity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets the descriptor for the top of the 3â4 band: structure and formatting conventions are correctly and clearly applied despite minor lapses, meriting the maximum score of 4.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student employs a reasonable range of evidence: primary text (film and screenplay), half-a-dozen academic or journalistic sources, plus several video essays.  Citations are generally appropriate and integrated, and the method of analysing characterisation through cinematic codes is explained.  However, reliance on popular media commentary and limited cross-checking of scholarly material shows only âmostly completeâ rather than fully robust methodology, matching the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Margins, font size, citation style, and visual material are generally well handled; word count, title statement, and pagination are provided.  A few caption misplacements and erratic line breaks appear, yet overall layout conventions are correctly applied, justifying a full 4 in this strand.

Source base is adequate: academic texts, statistical sites, popular criticism, and the primary film.  Most are clearly relevant to the focus, though a few (e.g.
Wikipedia entry on a song) add limited scholarly value.  Overall quality and alignment with the question sit comfortably in the mid-range of the 4-6 band.

The student states the overall purposeâto examine the filmâs warning about rape culture through characterisationâimmediately and reiterates it through section headings and topic sentences.  The focus on four character groupings provides a clear organising principle that is sustained throughout, demonstrating a consistently articulated purpose.  Minor digressions (e.g., lengthy plot recount) occasionally blur precision, so the strand sits low-to-mid in the 5-6 band rather than at the ceiling.

The essay advances a coherent line of argument across the four character sections and ends with an integrative conclusion.  Evaluation of broader social implications appears, but counter-arguments and critical weighing of sources are only partially developed.  This aligns with the descriptor of âgood argument development with partially critical evaluationââsolidly within the 4-6 band.

The analysis links cinematic techniques to rape-culture commentary with reasonable depth, using specific scenes and quotations to support arguments.  However, some paragraphs drift into descriptive retelling and a few analytical leaps are asserted rather than demonstrated.  Conclusions are âmostlyâ but not always fully supported, meriting a mid-band score of 5.

Required structural elements (title page, contents, introduction, logically sequenced parts, conclusion, works cited) are all present and functional.  Minor formatting lapses (e.g., inconsistent figure captions) do not disrupt reader navigation, so the work meets âclearly appropriateâ expectations at the top of the 3-4 band.

The essay reveals solid knowledge of rape-culture discourse and cinematic characterisation.  Definitions of concepts such as ânice-guy tropeâ and victim blaming are accurate, and the student demonstrates familiarity with film terminology (camera angle, costume, soundtrack).  Nevertheless, some claims rest on generalised social assertions without deep scholarly backing, preventing the work from reaching the âexcellent/coherentâ standard; thus a secure 4 is appropriate.

Subject-specific lexisââindirect characterisation,â âdiegetic sound,â âvictim-blaming,â âallusionââis regularly and mostly accurately applied.  A few slips (e.g., treating mise-en-scÃ¨ne solely as costume) limit consistency, keeping the performance within the âmostly accurateâ 3-4 band rather than fully âconsistentâ.

The research question â âHow and to what extent is characterisation used to warn society of the impacts of rape culture in Fennellâs Promising Young Woman?â â is clearly worded, narrow enough to be manageable and tightly linked to the ensuing discussion of specific cinematic techniques.  Although the phrase âto what extentâ is not fully unpacked analytically, the connection between question and essay remains strong, justifying placement in the 5-6 band at 5 marks.

annotations.comment: The outline of four character-focused parts provides a coherent organising principle that sustains clear purpose.
Reflection on the research process is descriptive rather than evaluative. Include insights on how specific setbacks informed methodological adjustments.
You note challenges in organization; strengthen this by referencing concrete planning tools (e.g., outlines, Gantt charts) to show reflexive practice.
The initial rationale outlines interest but lacks a precise methodological focus. Specify which analytical frameworks and source types will be employed.
The title and research question are clearly stated, focused, and tightly linked to the subsequent analysis.
Table of Contents is well-organized with clear headings, supporting reader navigation.
The introduction succinctly situates the film and frames the research question, demonstrating clear contextual understanding.
Word count is provided and falls within the maximum limit, fulfilling formatting requirements.
Consider listing the specific cinematic codes (e.g., camera angle, costume, soundtrack) and explaining their analytical significance.
The lengthy plot summary dilutes analytical focus. Trim narrative detail to prioritize critical engagement with characterisation.
The methodology description ends abruptly and lacks detail on how cinematic codes were systematically applied.
Citation of film timestamps is inconsistent; standardize format (e.g., $$\textit{Promising Young Woman}$$ 00:36:00) to improve presentation.
Insightful critique of casting choices demonstrates how rhetorical devices reinforce thematic claims.
Claim about viewersâ subconscious associations needs empirical support; cite a reception study or theory.
Allusion to mythological Cassandra shows sophisticated intertextual linkage and deep subject knowledge.
Part 1 topic sentence precisely defines the scope of victim portrayal analysis, guiding focused argumentation.
Deepen the analysis by linking the contrasting perceptions of Nina directly back to your central research question.
Challenging the revenge trope with Cassieâs response shows critical depth and supports well-grounded conclusions.
Relying on a Wikipedia entry for legal context weakens scholarly rigor; replace with a primary legal or academic source.
Closing sentence of Part 1 effectively summarizes findings but could link forward to Part 2 to improve flow.
Section header clearly states the analytical focus on Al Monroe, enhancing navigability.
Definition of the ânice guyâ trope relies on a popular press source; support with a peer-reviewed study on gendered behavior.
Strong integration of visual analysis with textual argument demonstrates effective multimodal understanding.
Standardize timestamp and figure references using consistent LaTeX formatting (e.g., $$\textit{Promising Young Woman}$$).
Citing Wikipedia for soundtrack analysis undermines academic rigor; reference a musicology or film studies source.
The split heading on Part 3 disrupts visual flow; integrate into a single line for clarity.
When discussing religious imagery, include precise timestamps or frame numbers to substantiate claims.
Works Cited entries show inconsistent URL and DOI formatting; adopt a uniform citation style throughout.
The conclusion effectively synthesizes cinematic techniques and asserts the filmâs broader social imperative.
Indentation and list formatting in the final reference list are uneven; ensure consistent presentation of list items.
